title: Hypotheses, Riskiest Assumptions, and Disposable Prototypes · Claim Repair Board
summary: A preserved decision record for hypotheses, riskiest assumptions, and disposable prototypes contains one plausible evidence, scope, design, implementation, evaluation, or ownership defect. Find the first unsupported claim before changing the product. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in ranked assumption map and prototype evidence and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal d…
objectives: Express desirable, usable, feasible, viable, ethical, accessible, secure, and operable assumptions as falsifiable claims.
Prioritize assumptions by uncertainty, consequence, dependency, and cheapest discriminating evidence.
Choose paper, clickable, coded, data, integration, or operational prototype fidelity to answer one question without production overinvestment.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Order the regression proof · Debug Prototype Disposal
steps.instruction:
Order the Prototype Disposal evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Order the regression proof · Debug Prototype Disposal.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Prototype Disposal assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Order the regression proof · Debug Prototype Disposal, keep Prototype Disposal connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A polished coded prototype appears persuasive, but it tests no predeclared assumption and quietly becomes production code after one positive comment. The debug variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Separate disposable exploration from production code and record what can and cannot transfer after the experiment.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Prototype Disposal has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Order the regression proof · Debug Prototype Disposal, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Prototype Disposal precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Order the regression proof · Debug Prototype Disposal, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A polished coded prototype appears persuasive, but it tests no predeclared assumption and quietly becomes production code after one positive comment. The debug variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Separate disposable exploration from production code and record what can and cannot transfer after the experiment.

For Order the regression proof · Debug Prototype Disposal, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A prototype that appears to work should become the production foundation.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints for the debug Prototype Disposal case
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure for the debug Prototype Disposal case
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence for the debug Prototype Disposal case
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise for the debug Prototype Disposal case

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Prototype Disposal
steps.instruction:
Append a pp1-prototype-disposal-16315g: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Prototype Disposal.

content.code:
pp1-prototype-disposal-16315g:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

steps.title: Publish the incident record · Debug Prototype Disposal
steps.instruction:

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate disposable exploration from production code and record what can and cannot transfer after the experiment.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Assumption Register.
